On this page

How-To Guides ​

This section provides concise answers to common "How do I do that?" questions that often arise when using Stubborn Contract.

Available How-To Guides ​

  • Why Use Stubborn Contract? — What makes Stubborn Contract stand out as a CDC tool
  • How Can I Provide Dynamic Values to a Contract? — Using matchers and generators for dynamic request/response fields
  • How Can I Debug the Mapping, Request, or Response Being Sent by WireMock? — Enabling verbose WireMock logging
  • How Can I Use Git as the Storage for Contracts and Stubs? — Using a Git repository as a contract/stub repository
  • How Can I Use a Common Repository with Contracts Instead of Storing with the Producer? — Hosting contracts in a shared repository
  • How to Do Stubs Versioning? — API versioning, JAR versioning, and dev/prod stubs
  • How Can I Mark that a Contract Is in Progress? — Managing in-progress contracts
  • How Can I Work with Transitive Dependencies? — Managing transitive dependencies in stubs

Getting Started ​

If you're new to Stubborn Contract, start with the Getting Started guide.

Reference Documentation ​

For comprehensive reference documentation, see the Reference section.
